drills.title: Tissue Test
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US retail market for facial tissues, in dollars.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=5 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: market size → what it means for entry/launching the line.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$100M, $1.6B]
- P1: ≥2 consumption occasions named (illness / background), not demography for demography's sake
- P2: all 5 anchors stated as numbers BEFORE calculating
- P3: second path (e.g. supermarket shelf or spend per household) within 2× + most reliable one named with justification
- P4: translation into a decision (10% share = $40M revenue — does it justify launching the line)
- S: normalization out loud — «$400M / 320M ≈ $1.25 per person per year» → plausibility verdict
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$100M, $1.6B]
- P1: названы ≥2 повода потребления (болезнь / фон), не демография ради демографии
- P2: все 5 якорей озвучены числом ДО расчёта
- P3: второй путь (напр. супермаркет-полка или расход на домохозяйство) в пределах 2× + назван надёжнейший с обоснованием
- P4: перевод в решение (доля 10% = $40M выручки — оправдывает ли запуск линии)
- S: нормировка вслух — «$400M / 320M ≈ $1.25 на человека в год» → вердикт правдоподобия

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • US population: ~320M [A1]
  • Share of regular users: ~10% [A1]
  • Colds per year / duration / tissues per day: 2 · 5 days · 20 [A4]
  • Tissues for other occasions (allergies): ~50/year [A4]
  • Tissues per box: 100 [A5]
  • Price per box: $5 [A5]

Reference chain:
users  = 320_000_000 * D('0.10')          # 32,000,000
per    = 2*5*20 + 50                       # 250 tissues/person/year
tissues= users * per                       # 8,000,000,000
boxes  = tissues / 100                     # 80,000,000
market = boxes * 5                         # $400,000,000

Reference answer: $400,000,000 · Corridor: [$100,000,000 … $1,600,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Население США: ~320M [A1]
  • Доля регулярных пользователей: ~10% [A1]
  • Простуд в год / длительность / салфеток в день: 2 · 5 дней · 20 [A4]
  • Салфеток на прочие поводы (аллергия): ~50/год [A4]
  • Салфеток в коробке: 100 [A5]
  • Цена коробки: $5 [A5]

Эталонная цепочка:
users  = 320_000_000 * D('0.10')          # 32,000,000
per    = 2*5*20 + 50                       # 250 салфеток/чел/год
tissues= users * per                       # 8,000,000,000
boxes  = tissues / 100                     # 80,000,000
market = boxes * 5                         # $400,000,000

Эталон: $400,000,000 · Коридор: [$100,000,000 … $1,600,000,000]

provoked.en: counting the entire population as consumers and taking an «average» frequency without splitting illness/non-illness. This overstates by an order of magnitude and, crucially, hides the driver — consumption is concentrated in illness episodes, not spread evenly.
provoked.ru: считать всё население потребителями и брать «среднюю» частоту без разделения болезнь/не-болезнь. Даёт завышение на порядок и, главное, скрывает драйвер — потребление сконцентрировано в эпизодах болезни, а не размазано.

drills.title: Households, Not Heads
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US market for automatic dishwasher detergent.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: conclusion in terms of market share and the feasibility of entry.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$3.03B, $27.3B]
- P1: counting base named explicitly (households) and justified by the nature of the product
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before calculating
- P3: second path (per-unit: households with a machine 70% · 5 cycles/week · 52 · $0.25/tablet = $5,915,000,000, ratio 1.54×) within 2× + most reliable one chosen
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$9.1B / 130M hh ≈ $70 per household per year ≈ $6/month» → does it reconcile with pack price and wash frequency
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$3.03B, $27.3B]
- P1: база счёта названа явно (домохозяйства) и обоснована природой товара
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь (штучный: домохозяйств с машиной 70% · 5 циклов/нед · 52 · $0.25/таблетка = $5,915,000,000, ratio 1.54×) в пределах 2× + выбор надёжнейшего
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$9.1B / 130M дх ≈ $70 на домохозяйство в год ≈ $6/мес» → сходится ли с ценой упаковки и частотой стирок

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • US households: ~130M [A1]
  • Average household income: ~$70,000 [A2]
  • Share of income on this category: ~0.1% [A4]

Reference chain:
market = 130_000_000 * 70_000 * D('0.001')   # 9,100,000,000

Reference answer: $9,100,000,000 · Corridor: [$3,033,333,333 … $27,300,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Домохозяйств в США: ~130M [A1]
  • Средний доход домохозяйства: ~$70,000 [A2]
  • Доля дохода на эту категорию: ~0.1% [A4]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 130_000_000 * 70_000 * D('0.001')   # 9,100,000,000

Эталон: $9,100,000,000 · Коридор: [$3,033,333,333 … $27,300,000,000]

provoked.en: counting by people (330M × spend per person). A dishwasher is a **household asset**, not a person's; counting by heads overstates the base by ~2.5× and breaks the very logic of the purchase.
provoked.ru: считать по людям (330M × расход на человека). Посудомоечная машина — **актив домохозяйства**, а не человека; счёт по головам завышает базу в ~2.5× и ломает саму логику покупки.

drills.title: Installed Base
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US market for residential refrigerators, in dollars.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: what the flow size means for capacity planning.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$3.9B, $35.3B]
- P1: stock (installed base) and flow (annual sales) explicitly separated
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers; service life named before calculating
- P3: second path (e.g. new construction + replacement separately) within 2× + choice
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$11.8B / 130M hh ≈ $90 per year per household at a $600 price — a purchase once every ~7 years» → does it reconcile
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$3.9B, $35.3B]
- P1: явно разделены запас (парк) и поток (годовые продажи)
- P2: 3 якоря числом; срок службы назван до расчёта
- P3: второй путь (напр. новое строительство + замена отдельно) в пределах 2× + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$11.8B / 130M дх ≈ $90 в год на домохозяйство при цене $600 — покупка раз в ~7 лет» → сходится ли

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Refrigerator fleet (≈households): ~280M [A1]
  • Service life: ~14 years → replacement ~7%/year [A4]
  • Average price: ~$600 [A5]

Reference chain:
market = 280_000_000 * D('0.07') * 600     # 11,760,000,000

Reference answer: $11,760,000,000 · Corridor: [$3,920,000,000 … $35,280,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Парк холодильников (≈домохозяйств): ~280M [A1]
  • Срок службы: ~14 лет → замена ~7%/год [A4]
  • Средняя цена: ~$600 [A5]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 280_000_000 * D('0.07') * 600     # 11,760,000,000

Эталон: $11,760,000,000 · Коридор: [$3,920,000,000 … $35,280,000,000]

provoked.en: equating the market to installed base × price ($168B) — that is, counting **stock as flow**. The annual market = replacement + growth only, not the entire installed base.
provoked.ru: приравнять рынок к парку × цену ($168B) — то есть посчитать **запас как поток**. Годовой рынок = только замена + прирост, а не весь установленный парк.

drills.title: Two Ways to Count
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US market for coffee sold in coffee shops (out-of-home), in dollars. Then estimate it a second way, from the supply side, and reconcile.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: which of the paths to take into the business case and why.

checklist.en:
- GATE: main path ∈ [$6.76B, $108.1B]
- P1: segmentation by occasion/frequency, not cosmetic
- P2: 4 anchors of the main path + 2 supply-side anchors as numbers before calculating
- P3: **second path independent (supply-side, not the same chain rearranged), divergence ≤2× (reference 1.73×), most reliable one named with justification**
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$27B / 330M ≈ $82 per person per year ≈ 18 cups» → does it reconcile with the stated frequency
checklist.ru:
- GATE: основной путь ∈ [$6.76B, $108.1B]
- P1: сегментация по поводу/частоте, не косметическая
- P2: 4 якоря основного пути + 2 якоря supply-side числом до расчёта
- P3: **второй путь независим (supply-side, не переставленная та же цепочка), расхождение ≤2× (эталон 1.73×), назван надёжнейший с обоснованием**
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$27B / 330M ≈ $82 на человека в год ≈ 18 чашек» → сходится ли с заявленной частотой

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • US population: ~330M [A1]
  • Share buying coffee out of home: ~35% [A1]
  • Frequency: ~1 time/week = 52/year [A4]
  • Average cup price: ~$4.50 [A5]
  • Coffee shops in the US: ~38,000 [A3]
  • Cups per day per coffee shop: ~250 [A3]

Reference chain:
bottom_up = 330_000_000 * D('0.35') * 52 * D('4.50')   # 27,027,000,000
supply    = 38_000 * 250 * 365 * D('4.50')             # 15,603,750,000
ratio     = bottom_up / supply                          # 1.73x  -> P3 passes (<=2x)

Reference answer: $27,027,000,000 · Corridor: [$6,756,750,000 … $108,108,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Население США: ~330M [A1]
  • Доля покупающих кофе вне дома: ~35% [A1]
  • Частота: ~1 раз/неделю = 52/год [A4]
  • Средняя цена чашки: ~$4.50 [A5]
  • Кофеен в США: ~38,000 [A3]
  • Чашек в день на кофейню: ~250 [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
bottom_up = 330_000_000 * D('0.35') * 52 * D('4.50')   # 27,027,000,000
supply    = 38_000 * 250 * 365 * D('4.50')             # 15,603,750,000
ratio     = bottom_up / supply                          # 1.73x  -> P3 проходит (<=2x)

Эталон: $27,027,000,000 · Коридор: [$6,756,750,000 … $108,108,000,000]

provoked.en: stopping at one path and declaring the result. The muscle of the drill is **where to get an independent second path**: not rearranging the multipliers of the same chain (that is not triangulation), but coming in from the supply side.
provoked.ru: остановиться на одном пути и объявить результат. Мышца дрилла — **откуда взять независимый второй путь**: не переставить множители той же цепочки (это не triangulation), а зайти со стороны предложения.

drills.title: The Whole Country?
drills.prompt: A government wants to sell insurance to underserved households. Aspirers: 80M households, ~$15,000 income, ~1.5% spent on insurance. Strugglers: 120M households, ~$5,000 income, ~1%. Within three years it expects to capture 33% of aspirers and 66% of strugglers. What is the addressable market it should plan against?
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: against which figure to plan the program budget.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$2.48B, $39.6B]
- P1: segments not mixed, capture applied to each separately
- P2: 4 anchors as numbers before calculating
- P3: second path (e.g. from the number of post offices × agents × average policy) ≤2× + choice
- P4: **the recommendation features SAM, not TAM** (otherwise P4 is not counted)
- S: «$9.9B over 200M households ≈ $50 premium per year» → plausible for these incomes
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$2.48B, $39.6B]
- P1: сегменты не смешаны, capture применён к каждому отдельно
- P2: 4 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь (напр. от числа почтовых отделений × агентов × средний полис) ≤2× + выбор
- P4: **в рекомендации фигурирует SAM, а не TAM** (иначе P4 не зачтён)
- S: «$9.9B на 200M домохозяйств ≈ $50 премии в год» → правдоподобно для этих доходов

provoked.en: planning against a TAM of $24B. Capture rate is not «who uses it at all» (that is already cut off by the choice of the two segments), but **reachability**: how much the state will actually capture over three years. Planning against TAM overstates the base by 2.4×.
provoked.ru: планировать против TAM $24B. Capture rate — не «кто вообще пользуется» (это уже отсечено выбором двух сегментов), а **достижимость**: сколько государство реально захватит за три года. Планирование против TAM завышает базу в 2.4×.

drills.title: Short Is Right
drills.prompt: A supplier sells safety equipment to industrial plants. There are ~1,200 such plants in the country, each spending ~$85,000/year on this category. Your colleague says: "this is too simple, we should really break it down by plant size, region, and equipment type before we can trust any number." Estimate the market.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=2 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
State explicitly why further segmentation would NOT improve the estimate (do not manufacture false structure).
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: what share is realistic to take and what it yields.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$34M, $306M]
- P1: **candidate explicitly says that additional segmentation is not needed and justifies why** (for Clean this is exactly what earns P1; building a fake segmentation = P1 not counted)
- P2: 2 anchors as numbers
- P3: second path (e.g. from the number of equipment units per plant × price) ≤2× + choice
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$102M over 1,200 plants = $85k — matches the input anchor; check on an independent base: ~$85k at a typical plant budget» → verdict
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$34M, $306M]
- P1: **кандидат явно говорит, что дополнительная сегментация не нужна, и обосновывает почему** (для Clean это и есть зачёт P1; построение фальшивой сегментации = P1 не зачтён)
- P2: 2 якоря числом
- P3: второй путь (напр. от числа единиц оборудования на завод × цена) ≤2× + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$102M на 1,200 заводов = $85k — совпадает с входным якорем; проверка на независимой базе: ~$85k при типичном бюджете завода» → вердикт

provoked.en: giving in to the colleague's hint and building a three-tier segmentation which, when assembled, gives the same number but eats time and adds error at each node.
provoked.ru: поддаться на подсказку коллеги и построить трёхуровневую сегментацию, которая при сборке даёт то же число, но съедает время и добавляет ошибку в каждом узле.

steps.prompt: State why no further segmentation is needed + one-sentence recommendation
steps.answer: reference answer $102,000,000; further segmentation adds no accuracy

drills.title: Firms, Not People
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual market for payroll-processing software sold to mid-sized companies in one country.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: conclusion about the addressable segment and entry.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$540M, $4.86B]
- P1: base = firms, named explicitly; non-addressable share cut off
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before calculating
- P3: second path (bottom-up: workers × price per payroll run per month) ≤2× + choice
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$1.62B / 45,000 firms ≈ $36k per firm per year» → is it plausible at a $120k spend
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$540M, $4.86B]
- P1: база = фирмы, названа явно; отсечена неадресуемая доля
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь (снизу: работников × цена за расчёт в месяц) ≤2× + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$1.62B / 45,000 фирм ≈ $36k на фирму в год» → правдоподобно ли при расходе $120k

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Mid-sized companies: ~45,000 [A3]
  • Payroll spend per year: ~$120,000 [A3]
  • Share outsourced to software/service: ~30% [A4]

Reference chain:
market = 45_000 * 120_000 * D('0.30')     # 1,620,000,000

Reference answer: $1,620,000,000 · Corridor: [$540,000,000 … $4,860,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Компаний среднего размера: ~45,000 [A3]
  • Расход на расчёт зарплаты в год: ~$120,000 [A3]
  • Доля, отдаваемая на аутсорс/софт: ~30% [A4]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 45_000 * 120_000 * D('0.30')     # 1,620,000,000

Эталон: $1,620,000,000 · Коридор: [$540,000,000 … $4,860,000,000]

provoked.en: coming in from population/number of employed («N workers × price per payroll run»). The buyer here is a **firm**, not a worker; counting from population loses the fact that the decision and budget belong to the company, and does not allow cutting off the share that stays in-house.
provoked.ru: зайти от населения/числа занятых («N работников × цена за расчёт»). Покупатель здесь — **фирма**, а не работник; счёт от населения теряет то, что решение и бюджет принадлежат компании, и не даёт отсечь долю, которая остаётся in-house.

drills.title: Backwards
drills.prompt: An analyst claims the US almond-snack market is $36B a year, at about $8 per pack. A regular buyer goes through roughly 60 packs a year. How many regular buyers does that claim imply — and is the claim plausible?
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the answer in the required units.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: can you build a business case on someone else's figure of $36B.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [25M, 225M] buyers
- P1: direction of the count named explicitly (from market to people), divisors listed
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers
- P3: second path (direct: population × share × frequency × price) ≤2× + choice
- P4: **verdict on the plausibility of the original claim** (not just a number)
- S: «75M ≈ 23% of the population, each buying a pack a week» → plausible/on the edge
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [25M, 225M] покупателей
- P1: направление счёта названо явно (от рынка к людям), делители перечислены
- P2: 3 якоря числом
- P3: второй путь (прямой: население × доля × частота × цена) ≤2× + выбор
- P4: **вердикт о правдоподобии исходного заявления** (не просто число)
- S: «75M ≈ 23% населения, каждый по пачке в неделю» → правдоподобно/на грани

reference.en:
Reference chain:
packs  = 36_000_000_000 / 8       # 4,500,000,000 packs
buyers = packs / 60               #    75,000,000 people

Reference answer: 75,000,000 buyers · Corridor: [25,000,000 … 225,000,000]
reference.ru:
Эталонная цепочка:
packs  = 36_000_000_000 / 8       # 4,500,000,000 пачек
buyers = packs / 60               #    75,000,000 человек

Эталон: 75,000,000 покупателей · Коридор: [25,000,000 … 225,000,000]

provoked.en: accepting $36B as a given and not checking what follows from it. The reverse move shows: the claimed market requires **75M regular buyers ≈ 23% of the US population**, each buying 60 packs a year. This is the upper bound of plausibility — the figure is not absurd, but not harmless either; the candidate is obliged to name this.
provoked.ru: принять $36B как данность и не проверить, что из неё следует. Обратный ход показывает: заявленный рынок требует **75M регулярных покупателей ≈ 23% населения США**, каждый по 60 пачек в год. Это верхняя граница правдоподобия — цифра не абсурдна, но и не безобидна; кандидат обязан это назвать.

steps.answer: reference answer 75,000,000 buyers; second path within ~2×

drills.title: Twice Counted
drills.prompt: A manufacturer sells industrial filters. It ships through 8,000 distributors, each moving about 2,500 units a year, at $40 per unit. Separately, end-users buy about 60,000,000 units a year at $0.80 average margin per unit to the manufacturer. A colleague adds both figures to size the market. Is that right?
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: which figure to carry into the sales plan.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$266.7M, $2.4B]
- P1: **explicitly named that the channels overlap**, and one slice chosen (P1 not counted if they are summed)
- P2: 3 anchors of the branch as numbers
- P3: second path (from end users: 60M × retail price) ≤2× + choice
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$800M / 20M units = $40 — matches the price; at 60M units the market would be $2.4B at the same price» → name which base is correct
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$266.7M, $2.4B]
- P1: **явно названо, что каналы пересекаются**, и выбран один срез (P1 не зачтён при сложении)
- P2: 3 якоря ветви числом
- P3: второй путь (от конечных пользователей: 60M × розничная цена) ≤2× + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$800M / 20M ед. = $40 — совпадает с ценой; на 60M ед. рынок был бы $2.4B по той же цене» → назвать, какая база верна

reference.en:
Exhibit:
| Channel | Volume | Economics |
|---|---|---|
| Through distributors | 8,000 × 2,500 units | $40 / unit |
| End users | 60,000,000 units | $0.80 margin / unit |

Reference chain:
b2b_branch = 8_000 * 2_500 * 40     # 800,000,000  <- market
b2c_figure = 60_000_000 * D('0.8')  #  48,000,000  <- THE SAME product, a different slice
# cannot be summed: 20,000,000 units through distributors are part of those same 60M units
correct = b2b_branch                 # 800,000,000
naive   = b2b_branch + b2c_figure    # 848,000,000

Reference answer: $800,000,000 · Corridor: [$266,666,667 … $2,400,000,000]
reference.ru:
Exhibit:
| Канал | Объём | Экономика |
|---|---|---|
| Через дистрибьюторов | 8,000 × 2,500 ед. | $40 / ед. |
| Конечные пользователи | 60,000,000 ед. | $0.80 маржи / ед. |

Эталонная цепочка:
b2b_branch = 8_000 * 2_500 * 40     # 800,000,000  <- рынок
b2c_figure = 60_000_000 * D('0.8')  #  48,000,000  <- ТА ЖЕ продукция, другой срез
# складывать нельзя: 20,000,000 ед. через дистрибьюторов и есть часть тех же 60M единиц
correct = b2b_branch                 # 800,000,000
naive   = b2b_branch + b2c_figure    # 848,000,000

Эталон: $800,000,000 · Коридор: [$266,666,667 … $2,400,000,000]

provoked.en: summing the two channels. This is **one and the same physical flow**, counted twice from different sides of the chain: the units going through distributors are part of the same units that end users buy. The error lives in the summation, not the multiplication — that is why k is computed on the branch (see §4.1).
provoked.ru: сложить два канала. Это **один и тот же физический поток**, посчитанный дважды с разных сторон цепочки: единицы, идущие через дистрибьюторов, входят в те же единицы, что покупают конечные пользователи. Ошибка живёт в сложении, не в умножении — поэтому и k считается по ветви (см. §4.1).

drills.title: Not Proportional
drills.prompt: The US market for a mid-priced consumer appliance is about $12B a year. A colleague scales it to a country with 75M people: "US has 330M, so it's 75/330 of the US — about $2.7B." The target country's income per capita is roughly 25% of the US level. Estimate the market properly.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: whether it is worth entering this market at such capacity.

checklist.en:
- GATE: result ∈ [$281.3M, $2.53B]
- P1: scaling base named explicitly (income, not population) and justified
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before calculating
- P3: second path (bottom-up: households × penetration × price) ≤2× + choice
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$844M / 75M ≈ $11 per person against $36 in the US at 25% income» → does it reconcile
checklist.ru:
- GATE: результат ∈ [$281.3M, $2.53B]
- P1: база масштабирования названа явно (доход, не население) и обоснована
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь (снизу: домохозяйства × penetration × цена) ≤2× + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$844M / 75M ≈ $11 на человека против $36 в США при доходе 25%» → сходится ли

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Target country population: ~75M [A1]
  • Income per capita relative to the US: ~25% [A2]
  • Category consumption per unit of income: ~$45/person at full income [A4]

Reference chain:
market = 75_000_000 * D('0.25') * 45      # 843,750,000

Reference answer: $843,750,000 · Corridor: [$281,250,000 … $2,531,250,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Население целевой страны: ~75M [A1]
  • Доход на душу относительно США: ~25% [A2]
  • Потребление категории на единицу дохода: ~$45/чел при полном доходе [A4]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 75_000_000 * D('0.25') * 45      # 843,750,000

Эталон: $843,750,000 · Коридор: [$281,250,000 … $2,531,250,000]

provoked.en: scaling by population (75/330 × $12B = $2.7B). For a mid-price-tier product the **proxy is nonlinear**: demand is determined by disposable income, not the number of people. The naive path overstates by ~3.2×. This is Hard precisely because the naive proportion looks mathematically flawless — the error is in the **choice of base**, not the arithmetic.
provoked.ru: масштабировать по населению (75/330 × $12B = $2.7B). Для товара средней ценовой категории **proxy нелинеен**: спрос определяется располагаемым доходом, а не числом людей. Наивный путь завышает в ~3.2×. Это Hard именно потому, что наивная пропорция выглядит математически безупречной — ошибка в **выборе базы**, а не в арифметике.

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):

  • Share with at least one subscription: ~55% [A1]
  • Average bill per month: ~$16 [A5]

Reference chain:
market = 130_000_000 * D('0.55') * 12 * 16      # 13,728,000,000

reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):

  • Доля с хотя бы одной подпиской: ~55% [A1]

Эталон: $13,728,000,000 · Коридор: [$4,576,000,000 … $41,184,000,000]

provoked.ru: считать по людям (330M × цена). Подписка делится внутри домохозяйства — один аккаунт на семью; счёт по головам завышает базу в ~2.5× и теряет то, что решение о покупке принимается на уровне домохозяйства.

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • US population: ~330M [A1]
  • Share of consumers: ~60% [A1]
  • Servings per year (averaged across seasons): ~25 [A4]
  • Price per serving: ~$3.50 [A5]

reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):

  • Доля потребителей: ~60% [A1]
  • Порций в год (усреднённо по сезонам): ~25 [A4]
  • Цена порции: ~$3.50 [A5]

Эталон: $17,325,000,000 · Коридор: [$4,331,250,000 … $69,300,000,000]

provoked.en: taking the summer frequency (2–3 times a week) and multiplying by 52 weeks. A seasonal product: the peak holds for ~3 months, the other nine consumption is several times lower. Extrapolating the peak overstates the annual frequency by 3–4×.
provoked.ru: взять летнюю частоту (2–3 раза в неделю) и умножить на 52 недели. Сезонный товар: пик держится ~3 месяца, остальные девять потребление в разы ниже. Экстраполяция пика завышает годовую частоту в 3–4×.

Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):

  • Trips per year per user: ~30 [A4]

provoked.en: counting in a subscription unit («N subscribers × $X/month»), whereas car-sharing revenue is transactional — you pay per trip, and the subscription merely waives the commission. The sales unit is chosen incorrectly, and the model loses the main driver (usage frequency).
provoked.ru: считать в юните подписки («N подписчиков × $X/мес»), тогда как выручка каршеринга транзакционная — платят за поездку, а подписка лишь снимает комиссию. Юнит продажи выбран неверно, и модель теряет главный драйвер (частоту использования).

reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Парк легковых авто: ~280M [A1]
  • Доля EV **через 5 лет**: ~12% [A3]
  • кВтч на EV в год (публичная зарядка): ~3,000 [A3]
  • Цена кВтч на публичной станции: ~$0.35 [A5]
  • *Контекст (не якорь):* доля EV сегодня: ~1% [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 280_000_000 * D('0.12') * 3_000 * D('0.35')   # 35,280,000,000

Эталон: $35,280,000,000 · Коридор: [$8,820,000,000 … $141,120,000,000]

provoked.en:
taking today's market and multiplying it by fleet growth linearly, or extrapolating today's 1% share at a «plus the same amount each year» rate. Penetration of a new technology follows an S-curve: over 5 years the share grows not linearly, and the projection base is the **future EV fleet**, not today's charging market.
*Note on k:* penetration is set by a **target level at the horizon** (12%), not by a growth rate. If it were set via CAGR, a fifth anchor would appear (today's base + rate) — this we deliberately avoid.
provoked.ru:
взять сегодняшний рынок и умножить на рост парка линейно, либо экстраполировать сегодняшнюю долю 1% темпом «плюс столько же в год». Penetration новой технологии идёт по S-кривой: за 5 лет доля растёт не линейно, а база проекции — **будущий парк EV**, а не сегодняшний рынок зарядки.
*Замечание по k:* penetration задан **целевым уровнем на горизонте** (12%), а не темпом роста. Если задавать через CAGR, появился бы пятый якорь (сегодняшняя база + темп) — этого сознательно избегаем.

drills.title: How Good Is That Number
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual market for private tutoring in one country. Then state which of your anchors your answer depends on most, and give your final answer accordingly.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: whether a decision can be made with a ×4 spread, and which anchor should be refined first.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$2.25B, $36B]
- P1: segmentation/estimation structure stated
- P2: 4 anchors as numbers **and each with a range** before the calculation
- P3: second path independent, ratio ≤2× (reference 1.32×) + choice
- P4: **final answer given as a range $4.5B–$18B, not a point**, and stated whether the spread changes the decision
- S: sensitivity run across **all four** anchors (not selectively) and the anchor with the maximum spread named — **checked by arithmetic: 4.00× versus 2.00× for the next one, the maximum is unique**
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$2.25B, $36B]
- P1: сегментация/структура оценки заявлена
- P2: 4 якоря числом **и с диапазоном каждый** до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим, ratio ≤2× (эталон 1.32×) + выбор
- P4: **финальный ответ подан диапазоном $4.5B–$18B, а не точкой**, и сказано, меняет ли разброс решение
- S: протяжка проведена по **всем четырём** якорям (не выборочно) и назван якорь с максимальным размахом — **проверяется арифметикой: 4.00× против 2.00× у ближайшего, максимум уникален**

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Employed in the country: 50,000,000 [±5%]
  • Share using the service: 15% [7.5%–30%]
  • Sessions per year: 30 [21–42]
  • Price per session: $40 [$36–46]

Reference chain:
market = 50_000_000 * D('0.15') * 30 * 40      # 9,000,000,000

Reference answer: $9,000,000,000 · Corridor: [$2,250,000,000 … $36,000,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Занятых в стране: 50,000,000 [±5%]
  • Доля пользующихся услугой: 15% [7.5%–30%]
  • Занятий в год: 30 [21–42]
  • Цена занятия: $40 [$36–46]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 50_000_000 * D('0.15') * 30 * 40      # 9,000,000,000

Эталон: $9,000,000,000 · Коридор: [$2,250,000,000 … $36,000,000,000]

provoked.en: giving a point estimate of $9B with equal confidence across all anchors. The anchors have **different quality**: the employed are known precisely, the share of users is a pure guess with a ×4 spread. A point answer hides that all the uncertainty sits in one place.
provoked.ru: дать точечную оценку $9B с одинаковой уверенностью по всем якорям. Якоря имеют **разное качество**: занятые известны точно, доля пользующихся — чистая догадка с разбросом ×4. Точечный ответ скрывает, что вся неопределённость сидит в одном месте.

drills.title: The Client Was Right
drills.prompt: A B2B software vendor tells you its addressable market is about **$540 million**: roughly 12,000 target companies at an average contract of $45,000 a year. Your engagement manager says: "clients always overstate their market — find where this is inflated." Check the estimate.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=2 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
State explicitly why further segmentation would NOT improve the estimate (do not manufacture false structure).
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: whether a sales plan can be built on the customer's figure.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$180M, $1.62B]
- P1: **the candidate explicitly confirms the estimate and justifies that no filter is needed** (inventing an arbitrary filter = P1 not credited)
- P2: 2 anchors checked for belonging to the correct base
- P3: second path independent (by seats), ratio ≤2× (reference 1.12×) + conclusion that the paths converge
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «$540M / 12,000 = $45,000 — matches; over 18 seats that's $2,500/seat per year» → plausible for B2B SaaS

*Clarification on the two seat prices:* **$2,500** — the price derived from the customer's contract ($45,000 ÷ 18 seats); **$2,800** — an independent market observation on which the second path is built. The values differ in origin and do not contradict each other — which is exactly why the paths diverge by 12% (ratio 1.12×) rather than collapse to zero.
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$180M, $1.62B]
- P1: **кандидат явно подтверждает оценку и обосновывает, что фильтр не нужен** (изобретение произвольного фильтра = P1 не зачтён)
- P2: 2 якоря проверены на принадлежность нужной базе
- P3: второй путь независим (по местам), ratio ≤2× (эталон 1.12×) + вывод, что пути сходятся
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$540M / 12,000 = $45,000 — совпадает; на 18 мест это $2,500/место в год» → правдоподобно для B2B SaaS

*Пояснение к двум ценам места:* **$2,500** — цена, вытекающая из контракта заказчика ($45,000 ÷ 18 мест); **$2,800** — независимое рыночное наблюдение, на котором строится второй путь. Величины разные по происхождению и не противоречат друг другу — именно поэтому пути расходятся на 12% (ratio 1.12×), а не совпадают в ноль.

provoked.en: giving in to the mindset «customers always overstate» and inventing a nonexistent filter (arbitrarily cutting the share of companies or the contract) in order to «find» an overstatement. This is the mirror of CM-046.
provoked.ru: поддаться установке «клиенты всегда завышают» и придумать несуществующий фильтр (произвольно срезать долю компаний или контракт), чтобы «найти» завышение. Это зеркало CM-046.

steps.prompt: State why no further segmentation is needed + one-sentence recommendation
steps.answer: reference answer $540,000,000; further segmentation adds no accuracy

drills.title: On and Off
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual market for beer in one country. Beer is sold both in bars and restaurants (on-trade) and in shops for home consumption (off-trade).
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: which channel to invest in — by volume or by revenue (they give different answers).

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$14.2B, $226.8B]
- P1: **the two branches counted separately, each with its own frequency and price. Averaging the channels (any single «average price») = P1 NOT credited**, even if the result lands in the corridor
- P2: 4 anchors as numbers (two per channel) before the calculation
- P3: second path independent (supply-side), ratio ≤2× (reference 1.45×) + choice
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «$56.7B / 81M drinkers ≈ $700 per year ≈ $13/week» → plausible; and «200 servings/year ≈ 4 per week»
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$14.2B, $226.8B]
- P1: **две ветви посчитаны раздельно, каждая со своей частотой и ценой. Усреднение каналов (любая единая «средняя цена») = P1 НЕ зачтён**, даже если результат попал в коридор
- P2: 4 якоря числом (по два на канал) до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (supply-side), ratio ≤2× (эталон 1.45×) + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$56.7B / 81M пьющих ≈ $700 в год ≈ $13/неделя» → правдоподобно; и «200 порций/год ≈ 4 в неделю»

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Population: ~180M [A1]
  • Share of beer drinkers: ~45% [A1]
  • Servings per year: on-trade / off-trade: ~60 / ~140 [A4]
  • Price per serving: on-trade / off-trade: $7 / $2 [A5]

Reference chain:
base      = 180_000_000 * D('0.45')                 # 81,000,000
on_trade  = base * 60  * 7                          # 34,020,000,000
off_trade = base * 140 * 2                          # 22,680,000,000
market    = on_trade + off_trade                    # 56,700,000,000

Reference answer: $56,700,000,000 · Corridor: [$14,175,000,000 … $226,800,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Население: ~180M [A1]
  • Доля пьющих пиво: ~45% [A1]
  • Порций в год: on-trade / off-trade: ~60 / ~140 [A4]
  • Цена порции: on-trade / off-trade: $7 / $2 [A5]

Эталонная цепочка:
base      = 180_000_000 * D('0.45')                 # 81,000,000
on_trade  = base * 60  * 7                          # 34,020,000,000
off_trade = base * 140 * 2                          # 22,680,000,000
market    = on_trade + off_trade                    # 56,700,000,000

Эталон: $56,700,000,000 · Коридор: [$14,175,000,000 … $226,800,000,000]

provoked.en: averaging the channels — taking the combined 200 servings and a **simple** average price of $4.50, yielding $72,900,000,000 (**1.29×, a 29% overstatement**). The mechanism: the **weighted** average price equals **$3.50**, because the cheap channel carries 140 of the 200 servings. The product of averages loses the covariance between frequency and price.
provoked.ru: усреднить каналы — взять суммарные 200 порций и **простую** среднюю цену $4.50, получив $72,900,000,000 (**1.29×, завышение 29%**). Механизм: **взвешенная** средняя цена равна **$3.50**, потому что дешёвый канал несёт 140 из 200 порций. Произведение средних теряет ковариацию между частотой и ценой.

drills.title: Four at a Time
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US replacement market for passenger car tyres.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: which flow to take into the sales plan and why OEM is counted separately.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$10.08B, $161.3B]
- P1: **the flow named explicitly (replacement, not OEM)**; adding OEM and replacement = P1 not credited
- P2: 5 anchors as numbers; the unit (set vs tyre) declared before the calculation
- P3: second path independent (supply-side), ratio ≤2× (reference 1.07×) + choice
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «0.30 sets per year = once every 3.3 years at 12,000 miles annual mileage and a 40,000 lifespan» → does it reconcile
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$10.08B, $161.3B]
- P1: **поток назван явно (замена, не OEM)**; сложение OEM и замены = P1 не зачтён
- P2: 5 якорей числом; юнит (комплект vs шина) объявлен до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (supply-side), ratio ≤2× (эталон 1.07×) + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «0.30 комплекта в год = раз в 3.3 года при пробеге 12,000 миль и ресурсе 40,000» → сходится ли

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Passenger car fleet: ~280M [A1]
  • Mileage per year: ~12,000 miles [A4]
  • Set lifespan: ~40,000 miles [A3]
  • Tyres per set: 4 [A3]
  • Price per tyre: ~$120 [A5]

Reference chain:
sets_per_car = D(12_000) / 40_000          # 0.30 sets/year = one set every 3.3 years
sets         = 280_000_000 * sets_per_car  # 84,000,000 sets
tyres        = sets * 4                    # 336,000,000 tyres
market       = tyres * 120                 # 40,320,000,000

Reference answer: $40,320,000,000 · Corridor: [$10,080,000,000 … $161,280,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Парк легковых авто: ~280M [A1]
  • Пробег в год: ~12,000 миль [A4]
  • Ресурс комплекта: ~40,000 миль [A3]
  • Шин в комплекте: 4 [A3]
  • Цена шины: ~$120 [A5]

Эталонная цепочка:
sets_per_car = D(12_000) / 40_000          # 0.30 комплекта/год = комплект раз в 3.3 года
sets         = 280_000_000 * sets_per_car  # 84,000,000 комплектов
tyres        = sets * 4                    # 336,000,000 шин
market       = tyres * 120                 # 40,320,000,000

Эталон: $40,320,000,000 · Коридор: [$10,080,000,000 … $161,280,000,000]

provoked.en: adding the OEM supply (tyres on new cars) to the replacement market. These are **different flows**, but both are measured in tyres and are easily summed; yet the question was asked only about replacement. The second typical error is counting in sets while taking the price per tyre (or vice versa), losing the multiplier of 4.
provoked.ru: сложить OEM-поставку (шины на новые авто) с рынком замены. Это **разные потоки**, но оба измеряются в шинах и легко суммируются; вопрос при этом задан только про replacement. Вторая типичная ошибка — считать в комплектах, а цену брать за шину (или наоборот), теряя множитель 4.

drills.title: Rich Country, Small Market
drills.prompt: The US market for premium skincare is about $14B a year (population ~330M). Estimate the market in a country of 50M people whose income per capita is about 60% of the US level.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: whether it is worth entering at such a capacity.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$350M, $3.15B]
- P1: scaling base named explicitly (income, not population) and justified by the premium nature of the category
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before the calculation
- P3: second path independent (retail), ratio ≤2× (reference 1.22×) + choice
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «$1.05B / 50M ≈ $21 per person versus $42 in the US» → does it reconcile with 60% income
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$350M, $3.15B]
- P1: база масштабирования названа явно (доход, не население) и обоснована премиальностью категории
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (розница), ratio ≤2× (эталон 1.22×) + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$1.05B / 50M ≈ $21 на человека против $42 в США» → сходится ли с доходом 60%

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Population of the target country: ~50M [A1]
  • Income per capita relative to the US: ~60% [A2]
  • Category consumption per person at full income: ~$35 [A4]

Reference chain:
market = 50_000_000 * D('0.60') * 35        # 1,050,000,000

Reference answer: $1,050,000,000 · Corridor: [$350,000,000 … $3,150,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Население целевой страны: ~50M [A1]
  • Доход на душу относительно США: ~60% [A2]
  • Потребление категории на человека при полном доходе: ~$35 [A4]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 50_000_000 * D('0.60') * 35        # 1,050,000,000

Эталон: $1,050,000,000 · Коридор: [$350,000,000 … $3,150,000,000]

provoked.en: scaling strictly by population (50/330 × $14B = $2.12B), ignoring income. For a **premium** category the link to income is stronger than to the number of people: at 60% income the per-capita consumption falls not proportionally but more steeply. The naive path overstates by ~2×.
provoked.ru: масштабировать строго по населению (50/330 × $14B = $2.12B), игнорируя доход. Для **премиальной** категории связь с доходом сильнее, чем с числом людей: при доходе 60% потребление на душу падает не пропорционально, а сильнее. Наивный путь завышает в ~2×.

drills.title: Second Owner
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US market for **used** passenger cars, in dollars.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: what the size of the secondary market means for the entry strategy.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$235B, $2.12T]
- P1: **base named explicitly — the fleet and its turnover, not new production** (counting from production = P1 not credited)
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before the calculation
- P3: second path independent (from dealers), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.80×) + choice of the most reliable
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «14% of the fleet per year = a car changes owner once every 7 years; over a 20-year lifespan that's ~3 owners» → does it reconcile
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$235B, $2.12T]
- P1: **база названа явно — парк и его оборачиваемость, а не выпуск новых** (счёт от выпуска = P1 не зачтён)
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (от дилеров), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.80×) + выбор надёжнейшего
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «14% парка в год = машина меняет владельца раз в 7 лет; при сроке жизни 20 лет это ~3 владельца» → сходится ли

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Passenger car fleet: ~280M [A1]
  • Share of the fleet changing owner per year: ~14% [A3]
  • Average transaction price: ~$18,000 [A5]

Reference chain:
market = 280_000_000 * D('0.14') * 18_000      # 705,600,000,000

Reference answer: $705,600,000,000 · Corridor: [$235,200,000,000 … $2,116,800,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Парк легковых авто: ~280M [A1]
  • Доля парка, меняющая владельца за год: ~14% [A3]
  • Средняя цена сделки: ~$18,000 [A5]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 280_000_000 * D('0.14') * 18_000      # 705,600,000,000

Эталон: $705,600,000,000 · Коридор: [$235,200,000,000 … $2,116,800,000,000]

provoked.en: counting the secondary market from **new-car production** («sold 15M new → used roughly the same»). The secondary market is fed not by production but by **fleet turnover**: one car changes owner 3–4 times over its life, so there are many times more transactions than new cars. The naive path understates by several times.
provoked.ru: считать вторичный рынок от **выпуска новых** авто («продали 15M новых → б/у примерно столько же»). Вторичный рынок питается не выпуском, а **оборачиваемостью парка**: один автомобиль за жизнь меняет владельца 3–4 раза, поэтому сделок кратно больше, чем новых машин. Наивный путь занижает в разы.

drills.title: Top Down, Bottom Up
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual market for corporate training delivered by external providers in one country. Build the estimate two ways — once from employees up, and once from the top down — and reconcile.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: which of the paths to carry into the business case and why.

checklist.en:
- GATE: main path ∈ [$10.8B, $172.5B]
- P1: segmentation by driver (who trains and who pays). *This drill's provocation is caught by P3, not P1 — see below*
- P2: 4 anchors of the main path as numbers before the calculation
- P3: **second path built from a DIFFERENT base (firms, not people)**, ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.13×); rearranging the multipliers of the same chain = P3 not credited
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «$43.1B / 160M employed ≈ $270 per worker per year» → does it reconcile with $1,400 spending at a 55% share and a 35% external share
checklist.ru:
- GATE: основной путь ∈ [$10.8B, $172.5B]
- P1: сегментация по драйверу (кто обучается и кто платит). *Провокация этого дрилла ловится P3, не P1 — см. ниже*
- P2: 4 якоря основного пути числом до расчёта
- P3: **второй путь построен от ДРУГОЙ базы (фирмы, не люди)**, ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.13×); перестановка множителей той же цепочки = P3 не зачтён
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$43.1B / 160M занятых ≈ $270 на работника в год» → сходится ли с расходом $1,400 при доле 55% и внешней доле 35%

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Employed in the country: ~160M [A1]
  • Share undergoing training per year: ~55% [A4]
  • Spending per trainee per year: ~$1,400 [A3]
  • Share given to external providers: ~35% [A4]
  • *For the second path:* firms in the country: ~6M [A3]
  • *For the second path:* employees per firm: ~30 [A3]
  • *For the second path:* spending per employee: ~$270 [A3]

Reference chain:
bottom_up = 160_000_000 * D('0.55') * 1_400 * D('0.35')   # 43,120,000,000
top_down  = 6_000_000 * 30 * 270                          # 48,600,000,000
ratio     = 1.13                                          # P3 ✓

Reference answer: $43,120,000,000 · Corridor: [$10,780,000,000 … $172,480,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Занятых в стране: ~160M [A1]
  • Доля проходящих обучение за год: ~55% [A4]
  • Расход на обучаемого в год: ~$1,400 [A3]
  • Доля, отдаваемая внешним провайдерам: ~35% [A4]
  • *Для второго пути:* фирм в стране: ~6M [A3]
  • *Для второго пути:* сотрудников на фирму: ~30 [A3]
  • *Для второго пути:* расход на сотрудника: ~$270 [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
bottom_up = 160_000_000 * D('0.55') * 1_400 * D('0.35')   # 43,120,000,000
top_down  = 6_000_000 * 30 * 270                          # 48,600,000,000
ratio     = 1.13                                          # P3 ✓

Эталон: $43,120,000,000 · Коридор: [$10,780,000,000 … $172,480,000,000]

provoked.en: taking as the second path the same chain with the multipliers rearranged («employed × spending» instead of «employed × share × spending»). This is **not triangulation** — the second chain is derived from the first by renaming. The muscle of the drill is to approach from **a different side**: from the number of firms and their budgets, not from people.
provoked.ru: взять вторым путём ту же цепочку, переставив множители («занятых × расход» вместо «занятых × доля × расход»). Это **не triangulation** — из первой цепочки вторая получается переименованием. Мышца дрилла — зайти с **другой стороны**: от числа фирм и их бюджетов, а не от людей.

drills.title: Machines or People
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual revenue of the ATM industry in one country (fees paid by banks and cardholders).
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=2 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: what this means for the decision to expand/contract the network.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$1.41B, $12.69B]
- P1: **the counting unit named explicitly (a machine) and justified**; counting by users instead of machines = P1 not credited
- P2: 2 anchors as numbers before the calculation
- P3: second path independent (via transactions), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.34×) + choice
- P4: translated into a decision
- S: «$4.23B / 470,000 = $9,000 per machine ≈ $750/month» → is this plausible for a self-service point
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$1.41B, $12.69B]
- P1: **юнит счёта назван явно (аппарат) и обоснован**; счёт по пользователям вместо аппаратов = P1 не зачтён
- P2: 2 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (через транзакции), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.34×) + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$4.23B / 470,000 = $9,000 на аппарат ≈ $750/мес» → правдоподобно ли для точки самообслуживания

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • ATMs in the country: ~470,000 [A3]
  • Revenue per ATM per year: ~$9,000 [A3]

Reference chain:
market = 470_000 * 9_000        # 4,230,000,000

Reference answer: $4,230,000,000 · Corridor: [$1,410,000,000 … $12,690,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Банкоматов в стране: ~470,000 [A3]
  • Выручка на банкомат в год: ~$9,000 [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 470_000 * 9_000        # 4,230,000,000

Эталон: $4,230,000,000 · Коридор: [$1,410,000,000 … $12,690,000,000]

provoked.en: counting by users («how many people withdraw cash»), whereas revenue is generated by the **installed machine** — it earns income regardless of how many different people used it. The counting unit here is the machine, not the person; counting by people loses the fact that network density and its utilization are different things.
provoked.ru: считать по пользователям («сколько людей снимает наличные»), тогда как выручку генерирует **установленный аппарат** — он приносит доход независимо от того, сколько разных людей им пользовались. Юнит счёта здесь — машина, а не человек; счёт по людям теряет то, что плотность сети и её загрузка суть разные вещи.

drills.title: How Much Is Ours
drills.prompt: A telemedicine company asks how large **its own** opportunity is. Estimate the market it can realistically capture.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=5 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: against which figure to plan revenue and hiring.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$87.8M, $1.4B]
- P1: **the three levels named separately (TAM/SAM/SOM)**, answer given at the SOM level; an answer at the SAM or TAM level = P1 not credited
- P2: 5 anchors as numbers before the calculation
- P3: second path independent (from the partner network), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.33×) + choice
- P4: **the recommendation features SOM, not SAM or TAM** (otherwise P4 not credited)
- S: «$351M / 15% share = $2.34B SAM; over 13M using households that's $180/year = $15/month» → does it reconcile
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$87.8M, $1.4B]
- P1: **три уровня названы раздельно (TAM/SAM/SOM)**, ответ дан на уровне SOM; ответ на уровне SAM или TAM = P1 не зачтён
- P2: 5 якорей числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (от партнёрской сети), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.33×) + выбор
- P4: **в рекомендации фигурирует SOM, а не SAM или TAM** (иначе P4 не зачтён)
- S: «$351M / 15% доли = $2.34B SAM; на 13M пользующихся дх это $180/год = $15/мес» → сходится ли

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Households: ~130M [A1]
  • Share with access (insurance covers telemedicine): ~40% [A3]
  • Share actually using it: ~25% [A4]
  • Spending per using household per year: ~$180 [A5]
  • Player's market share (competition): ~15% [A3]

Reference chain:
TAM = 130_000_000 * 180                                    # 23,400,000,000
SAM = 130_000_000 * D('0.40') * D('0.25') * 180            #  2,340,000,000
SOM = SAM * D('0.15')                                      #    351,000,000

Reference answer: $351,000,000 · Corridor: [$87,750,000 … $1,404,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Домохозяйств: ~130M [A1]
  • Доля с доступом (страховка покрывает телемедицину): ~40% [A3]
  • Доля реально пользующихся: ~25% [A4]
  • Расход на пользующееся домохозяйство в год: ~$180 [A5]
  • Доля рынка игрока (конкуренция): ~15% [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
TAM = 130_000_000 * 180                                    # 23,400,000,000
SAM = 130_000_000 * D('0.40') * D('0.25') * 180            #  2,340,000,000
SOM = SAM * D('0.15')                                      #    351,000,000

Эталон: $351,000,000 · Коридор: [$87,750,000 … $1,404,000,000]

provoked.en:
planning against the SAM $2.34B, forgetting that the market is shared with competitors. The three levels easily collapse into one: TAM $23.4B → SAM $2.34B (×10 difference) → SOM $351M (another ×6.7). The question was asked about **«our»** market — the SAM answer overstates by 6.7×.
*Difference from MS-029:* here the final filter is **the market share won from competitors**. In MS-029 the filter is a property of the object itself.
provoked.ru:
планировать против SAM $2.34B, забыв, что рынок делится с конкурентами. Три уровня легко схлопываются в один: TAM $23.4B → SAM $2.34B (×10 разница) → SOM $351M (ещё ×6.7). Вопрос задан про **«наш»** рынок — ответ SAM завышает в 6.7×.
*Отличие от MS-029:* здесь последний фильтр — **доля рынка, отвоёванная у конкурентов**. В MS-029 фильтр — свойство самого объекта.

drills.title: Share of Wallet
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual market for enterprise cybersecurity products and services in one country.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: which segment within the category to address.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [$3.53B, $31.75B]
- P1: **base is named as a share of someone else's budget**, category coverage (products + services) is specified; counting via own units (licenses × price) = P1 not credited
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before the calculation
- P3: second path is independent (via the labor market), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.21×) + choice
- P4: translation into a decision
- S: «$10.58B / 28,000 firms = $378,000 per firm per year» → does it reconcile with the IT budget $4.2M
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [$3.53B, $31.75B]
- P1: **база названа как доля чужого бюджета**, охват категории (продукты + услуги) оговорён; счёт через собственные юниты (лицензии × цена) = P1 не зачтён
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (через рынок труда), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.21×) + выбор
- P4: перевод в решение
- S: «$10.58B / 28,000 фирм = $378,000 на фирму в год» → сходится ли с ИТ-бюджетом $4.2M

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Mid-sized and large firms: ~28,000 [A3]
  • IT budget per firm per year: ~$4.2M [A3]
  • Share of IT budget on security: ~9% [A3]

Reference chain:
market = 28_000 * 4_200_000 * D('0.09')      # 10,584,000,000

Reference answer: $10,584,000,000 · Corridor: [$3,528,000,000 … $31,752,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Средних и крупных фирм: ~28,000 [A3]
  • ИТ-бюджет на фирму в год: ~$4.2M [A3]
  • Доля ИТ-бюджета на безопасность: ~9% [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
market = 28_000 * 4_200_000 * D('0.09')      # 10,584,000,000

Эталон: $10,584,000,000 · Коридор: [$3,528,000,000 … $31,752,000,000]

provoked.en: counting «bottom-up» via own units (licenses × price), which yields only the product segment and loses services, integration and people — more than half the market. B2B sizing is more correctly built **from the share of someone else's budget**: how much the client spends on the category at all.
provoked.ru: считать «снизу» через свои юниты (лицензии × цена), что даёт только продуктовый сегмент и теряет услуги, интеграцию и людей — больше половины рынка. B2B-сайзинг корректнее строить **от доли чужого бюджета**: сколько клиент вообще тратит на категорию.

drills.title: Backwards to a Share
drills.focus: M14(b) от цели выручки к доле
drills.prompt: A manufacturer wants **$250 million** in annual revenue from a new home-appliance category and believes it can win a **4% share**. Is that consistent? Estimate the market and judge whether the target is reachable.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: whether to take the $250M target with no headroom on the share.

checklist.en:
- GATE: market estimate ∈ [$2.11B, $19.01B]
- P1: **the candidate estimates the market independently, rather than dividing the target by the share** (dividing the target by the share as «market estimate» = P1 not credited)
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before the calculation; the input (target, share) is not passed off as an estimate
- P3: second path is independent (from retail), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.48×) + choice
- P4: **verdict on achievability** (3.95% versus 4% — right at the edge, no headroom), not just a number
- S: «$6.34B / 9M hh ≈ $704 per year per household of the segment» → does it reconcile with the check $1,280 at 55%
checklist.ru:
- GATE: оценка рынка ∈ [$2.11B, $19.01B]
- P1: **кандидат оценивает рынок независимо, а не делит цель на долю** (деление цели на долю как «оценка рынка» = P1 не зачтён)
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта; вход (цель, доля) не выдаётся за оценку
- P3: второй путь независим (от розницы), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.48×) + выбор
- P4: **вердикт о достижимости** (3.95% против 4% — впритык, запаса нет), а не просто число
- S: «$6.34B / 9M дх ≈ $704 в год на домохозяйство сегмента» → сходится ли с чеком $1,280 при 55%

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Households in the target segment: ~9M [A1]
  • Share purchasing the category within a year: ~55% [A4]
  • Average check: ~$1,280 [A5]
  • *Input (not an anchor):* target revenue: $250M [—]
  • *Input (not an anchor):* stated share: 4% [—]

Reference chain:
market         = 9_000_000 * D('0.55') * 1_280       # 6,336,000,000
share_needed   = 250_000_000 / market                # 3.95%
# stated 4% -> target achievable, but right at the edge

Reference answer: $6,336,000,000 · Corridor: [$2,112,000,000 … $19,008,000,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Домохозяйств в целевом сегменте: ~9M [A1]
  • Доля, покупающая категорию за год: ~55% [A4]
  • Средний чек: ~$1,280 [A5]
  • *Вход (не якорь):* целевая выручка: $250M [—]
  • *Вход (не якорь):* заявленная доля: 4% [—]

Эталонная цепочка:
market         = 9_000_000 * D('0.55') * 1_280       # 6,336,000,000
share_needed   = 250_000_000 / market                # 3.95%
# заявленные 4% -> цель достижима, но впритык

Эталон: $6,336,000,000 · Коридор: [$2,112,000,000 … $19,008,000,000]

provoked.en: taking «$250M ÷ 4% = $6.25B» as a market estimate. This is **not an estimate** but a rephrasing of the target: it is arithmetically correct for any market and verifies nothing. The candidate's job is to **independently estimate the market** and compare it with the required share.
provoked.ru: принять «$250M ÷ 4% = $6.25B» за оценку рынка. Это **не оценка**, а перефразировка цели: она верна арифметически при любом рынке и ничего не проверяет. Работа кандидата — **независимо оценить рынок** и сопоставить с требуемой долей.

drills.title: How Many to Break Even
drills.prompt: A subscription service has **$52 million** in annual fixed costs. Price is **$420** per customer per year, variable cost **$160**. How many customers are needed to break even — and is that number achievable given the network can serve about 220,000?
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=3 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the answer in the required units.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
Sanity-check: normalize per capita / per household aloud and give a plausibility verdict.
Last mile: whether to launch at a required utilization of 91%.

checklist.en:
- GATE: ∈ [66,667, 600,000] customers
- P1: **the denominator is named explicitly as margin (price − variable)**; dividing by price = P1 not credited
- P2: 3 anchors as numbers before the calculation
- P3: second path is independent (from network capacity), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (reference 1.10×) + choice
- P4: **verdict on achievability at 91% utilization**, not just a number
- S: «200,000 customers × $260 margin = $52M — reconciles with fixed costs» → check by working backwards
checklist.ru:
- GATE: ∈ [66,667, 600,000] клиентов
- P1: **знаменатель назван явно как маржа (цена − переменные)**; деление на цену = P1 не зачтён
- P2: 3 якоря числом до расчёта
- P3: второй путь независим (от ёмкости сети), ratio ∈ [1.05×, 2.00×] (эталон 1.10×) + выбор
- P4: **вердикт о достижимости с загрузкой 91%**, а не просто число
- S: «200,000 клиентов × $260 маржи = $52M — сходится с постоянными затратами» → проверка обратным ходом

reference.en:
Allowed anchors (revealed on request, not upfront):
  • Fixed costs per year: $52M [A3]
  • Price per customer per year: $420 [A5]
  • Variable cost per customer: $160 [A3]
  • *For the second path:* service centers: ~210 [A3]
  • *For the second path:* customers per center: ~1,050 [A3]

Reference chain:
margin    = 420 - 160                    # 260 per customer
breakeven = 52_000_000 / 260             # 200,000 customers (exact integer)

Reference answer: 200,000 customers · Corridor: [66,667 … 600,000]
reference.ru:
Разрешённые якоря (раскрываются по запросу, не вперёд):
  • Постоянные затраты в год: $52M [A3]
  • Цена на клиента в год: $420 [A5]
  • Переменные затраты на клиента: $160 [A3]
  • *Для второго пути:* центров обслуживания: ~210 [A3]
  • *Для второго пути:* клиентов на центр: ~1,050 [A3]

Эталонная цепочка:
margin    = 420 - 160                    # 260 на клиента
breakeven = 52_000_000 / 260             # 200,000 клиентов (точное целое)

Эталон: 200,000 клиентов · Коридор: [66,667 … 600,000]

provoked.en: dividing fixed costs by **price** ($52M ÷ $420 = 123,810), ignoring variable costs. This understates breakeven by 1.6× and creates a false impression of large headroom: it seems you need 124k at a capacity of 220k (78% headroom), whereas in reality you need 200k (10% headroom).
provoked.ru: делить постоянные затраты на **цену** ($52M ÷ $420 = 123,810), игнорируя переменные затраты. Это занижает breakeven в 1.6× и создаёт ложное впечатление большого запаса: кажется, что нужно 124k при ёмкости 220k (запас 78%), тогда как реально нужно 200k (запас 10%).

steps.answer: reference answer 200,000 customers; second path within ~2×

drills.title: Every Two Years
drills.prompt: Estimate the annual US market for smartphones, in dollars.
step_prompts: Segment the market and state the counting base explicitly (people / households / firms / installed base) with a one-line hypothesis.
Name each anchor as a number before computing (k=4 estimate-anchors).
Compute the estimate along the main chain and state the market size in $.
Triangulate with an independent second path (a different data source, not the same chain reordered) and reconcile — gap should be within ~2×; name the more reliable path with justification.
